content.p: In addition to the land acquisitions authorized by part F, the Secretary may acquire other non-Federal lands and interests in lands located within the boundaries of the Cooperative Management and Protection Area or the Wilderness Area.
Lands may be acquired under this subsection only by voluntary exchange, donation, or purchase from willing sellers.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), lands or interests in lands acquired under subsection (a) or part F that are located within the boundaries of the Cooperative Management and Protection Area shall—

subparagraph.content: become part of the Cooperative Management and Protection Area; and
be managed pursuant to the laws applicable to the Cooperative Management and Protection Area.

paragraph.heading: Lands within Wilderness Area
paragraph.chapeau: If lands or interests in lands acquired under subsection (a) or part F are within the boundaries of the Wilderness Area, the acquired lands or interests in lands shall—

subparagraph.content: become part of the Wilderness Area; and
be managed pursuant to part B and the other laws applicable to the Wilderness Area.

paragraph.heading: Lands within wilderness study area
paragraph.chapeau: If the lands or interests in lands acquired under subsection (a) or part F are within the boundaries of a wilderness study area, the acquired lands or interests in lands shall—

subparagraph.content: become part of that wilderness study area; and
be managed pursuant to the laws applicable to that wilderness study area.

content.p: In appraising non-Federal land, development rights, or conservation easements for possible acquisition under this section or section 460nnn–42 of this title, the Secretary shall disregard any adverse impacts on values resulting from the designation of the Cooperative Management and Protection Area or the Wilderness Area.
